reasoning: 1. Baseline: The last known value before March 30 is 7.263. 2. Short-term (Apr 6, Apr 29): The FRED statistical model predicts a near-term dip (P(increase) ~0.31) due to possible mean reversion from the recent late-March spike (from 7.23 to 7.26+) and PBOC's steady fixing policy. I forecast 43% for Apr 6, shifting to 52% by late April as tariff pressures start mounting. 3. Medium-term (Jun 28): Market consensus (e.g., MUFG, XTransfer) anticipates USD/CNY to peak around mid-2025 (with targets as high as 7.4 to 7.5) as the market fully prices in new US tariffs and potential yield spread widening. This implies a higher probability of exceeding the 7.263 baseline, so I placed this at 58%. 4. Long-term (Sep 26, Mar 2026): Forecasters expect the USD to potentially weaken broadly in late 2025, which may allow the Yuan to recover. Combined with the rule that uncertainty should increase and probabilities should shrink toward 0.5 for more distant horizons, I project 54% and 51% respectively.
